message:
feat(fcp-raptorq): end-to-end payload hash to reject false-positive decodes

Adds an optional blake3 payload hash to ObjectTransmissionInformation
so the decoder can verify that the reassembled payload matches the
original data.  This catches rare false-positive decode events where
the Gaussian elimination succeeds numerically but produces incorrect
data — a known possibility with RaptorQ when receiving exactly K
symbols with marginal rank.

Encoder side:
- RaptorQEncoder now computes blake3::hash(payload) at construction
  and attaches it to the OTI via with_payload_hash().
- ObjectTransmissionInformation gains an optional payload_hash field
  with serde support (skip_serializing_if is_none, default on deser).

Decoder side:
- RaptorQDecoder carries the payload_hash from the OTI and, after
  successful reassembly + truncation, verifies the hash.  On mismatch
  it returns InsufficientSymbols to signal that more repair symbols
  are needed.
- Also fix clone_from(&data) -> clone_from(data) in the dense
  reconstruction path (removes unnecessary reference-to-reference).

Store integration:
- ObjectTransmissionInfo (the store-layer mirror of the raptorq OTI)
  gains the payload_hash field and round-trips it through from_oti()
  and to_oti().

Tests: OTI payload_hash serde round-trip, decoder rejects mismatched
hash, default OTI has no hash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
